zh.Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Corbet於1281年10月18日出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，他的父親Robert Corbet47歲，母親Matilda Arundel37歲。他於大約1303年與Amicia Hughes在英國英格蘭史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1310年5月7日在英國史洛普爾享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Corbet于1281年10月18日出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特时，他的父亲Robert Corbet47岁，母亲Matilda Arundel37岁。他于大约1303年与Amicia Hughes在英国英格兰史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1310年5月7日在英国史洛普爾享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Corbet was born on 18 October 1281, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Corbet, was 47 and his mother, Matilda Arundel, was 37. He married Amicia Hughes about 1303, in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 7 May 1310, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Corbet est né le 18 octobre 1281, à Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Corbet, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Matilda Arundel, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Amicia Hughes environ 1303, à Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 7 mai 1310, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 28 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Corbetが1281年10月18日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアモーエトン コルベットで生まれた時，父Robert Corbetは47歳，母Matilda Arundelは37歳であった。約1303年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアワットレスボロウでAmicia Hughesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1310年5月7日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Corbet(이)가 1281년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Corbet(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Arundel(은)는 37세였다. 그는 대략 1303년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 왓틀레스버루그h에서 Amicia Hughes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1310년 5월 7일 영국 슈럽실르에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Amicia Hughes aproximadamente 1303, em Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Corbet родился 18 октября 1281 г. в местности моретон корбет, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Corbet было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Arundel, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Amicia Hughes около 1303 г. в местности ваттлесбороую, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 7 мая 1310 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 28 лет.
